channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Sources: FIFA, FIFPRO in talks over heat protocol ...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA and global players' union FIFPRO are in discussions over establishing protocols to avoid games being played in excessive heat, sources have told ESPN.

item.title: Norway boss fumes at 'clear' TV wire goal assist
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Norway boss Stale Solbakken has said he believes the overhead wires deserve an assist for England's equaliser.

item.title: Messi praises 'not normal' Argentina in WC defense...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lionel Messi has said Argentina's remarkable run of success is "not normal" after the reigning world champions beat Switzerland to reach the World Cup semifinals, where they will face England.

item.title: Haaland Sr: Norway 'robbed' in QF, 'well done ref'...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Alf-Inge Haaland claims Norway were "robbed" in their World Cup quarterfinal but hopes England go on to win the tournament after knocking out his homeland.

item.title: Copy of Embolo red-carded for diving after VAR review
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Switzerland forward Breel Embolo was sent off in the second half of a World Cup quarterfinal match with Argentina on Saturday night after a video review showed he was guilty of simulation.

item.title: Bellingham backs England stars after Tuchel fury
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Jude Bellingham praised England's fighting spirit and ability to "win dirty" after coach Thomas Tuchel criticized their performance in beating Norway on Saturday.

item.title: FIFA denies ball hit wire in England's first goal
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA stated there is "no evidence" the ball struck an overhead wire before England's equalizing goal against Norway in the Three Lions' 2-1 World Cup quarterfinals victory.

item.title: Sources: Chelsea prioritise exit for Garnacho
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Chelsea are demanding €50 million ($57m) for Alejandro Garnacho after the winger did not report back to the club for preseason training, sources have told ESPN.

item.title: ⚽ USMNT World Cup grades: Expectations met?
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: At this summer's World Cup, the U.S. did its homework, aced the quizzes, and then failed the final exam. How should we grade its World Cup?

item.title: Magic from Álvarez, not Messi, sees Argentina thro...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: It wasn't Lionel Messi coming up with the goods, but Argentina's supporting cast stepped up after the Switzerland defense muted their captain.

item.title: World Cup VAR review: Embolo sent off as Ream's 'm...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: For the tournament, we are taking a look at the major incidents to examine and explain the process in terms of both VAR protocol and the laws of the game.

item.title: Get ready for the World Cup semifinals! What you n...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: With only a week to go until the final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, there are now just four teams still harboring dreams of becoming world champions.

item.title: Mbappe overtakes Messi in FIFA’s latest Golden Boot standings
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: France captain Kylian Mbappe has moved ahead of Argentina's Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot at the 2026 FIFA World Cup after FIFA released its latest top scorers' standings ahead of the semi-finals. The post Mbappe overtakes Messi in FIFA’s latest Golden Boot standings appeared first on

item.title: NPFL: Daniel Ogunmodede begins work at Kano Pillars
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Daniel Ogunmodede has resumed duties as technical adviser of Kano Pillars, DAILY POST reports. Ogunmodede took charge of his first training session with the players at the Sani Abacha Stadium tadium in Kano on Sunday. The 46-year-old was unveiled by the four-time Nigeria Premier Football League, NPF

item.title: Alavin retracts viral allegation against Wizkid associate
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I am not making this video under duress. We are all friends,” he added.

item.title: FG Hails SON Over Global Approval Of Root, Tuber Flour Standards
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has commended the Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON) for securing international approval for a new standard on root and tuber flours, describing the achievement as a major milestone that will strengthen Nigeria’s agricultural exports and global food trade competitiv

item.title: Former Nigeria U20 Coach Zubairu lands two-year Sudan deal
item.dc:creator: PM News

item.title: Nwabali returns to Chippa United five months after mutual exit

item.description: Super Eagles goalkeeper Stanley Nwabali has completed a return to South African side Chippa United ahead of the 2026/27 season. The post Nwabali returns to Chippa United five months after mutual exit appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has projected that Nigerians will face higher prices for essential goods in 2026, warning that The post IMF projects higher prices for essentials in Nigeria appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

item.description: Deputy President of the Senate, Senator Barau Jibrin, has described the proposed establishment of state police as The post State police will strengthen Nigeria’s security architecture — Senator Barau appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Royal Ascot hero Thesecretadversary scooped top honours in Deauville with a brilliant front-running success in the Prix Jean Prat.

item.description: As competition intensifies in Nigeria’s telecommunications industry, Globacom is strengthening its nationwide dealer network by rewarding top-performing partners while unveiling read more Globacom bets on dealers, AI to defend market share in fierce telecom battle

item.description: By Gabriel Ewepu, Abuja The Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security has announced plans to deploy the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC)’s digital identity infrastructure to verify genuine farmers and improve the targeting of government agricultural intervention programme

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday over an Iranian strike on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz read more US launches fresh strike against Iran after it said Strait of Hormuz is shut

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria’s telecommunications regulator is shifting attention from expanding network coverage to ensuring the industry’s long-term financial sustainability, as the Nigerian read more NCC targets fresh digital infrastructure investment as telecom forum focuses on sector survival

item.description: Telecel Group, the pan-African telecommunications company co-founded by Lebanese-Ghanaian businessman Moh Damush, is making a bold push into Europe, challenging read more African-owned Telecel challenges European rivals for UK network

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Rita Dominic is celebrating another year of life with gratitude and elegance. item.title: Nigerian government must rethink how it treats football – Edun

item.description: For decades, Nigeria has produced world-class athletes despite weak grassroots structures, poor infrastructure and inconsistent investment. While football continues to read more Nigerian government must rethink how it treats football – Edun

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Linus Aleke in Abuja Troops of the 65 Battalion, Nigerian Army, have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of illicit drugs along the Lagos–Calabar Coastal Road following actionable intelligence

item.description: The founder of the Living Faith Church (LFC), Bishop David Oyedepo, on Sunday urged worshippers to secure their Permanent Voter Cards (PVCs) ahead of the country’s 2027 general election. He

item.title: Kano Pilgrims Board Fixes 2027 Hajj Deposit At N8.5m
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kano State Pilgrims Welfare Board has announced an initial deposit of N8.5 million for intending pilgrims seeking to perform the 2027 Hajj. The Director-General of the Board, Alhaji Abubakar Ibrahim Matawalle, disclosed this during a press conference held at the board’s headquarters in Kan

item.title: Benue: Protesters Storm Och’Idoma’s Palace With Corpses After Weekend Herdsmen Attack
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Enraged protesters on Sunday stormed the palace of the paramount ruler of the Idoma nation, the Och’Idoma, His Royal Majesty Agabaidu Elaigwu John Odogbo, carrying the corpses of victims killed in fresh attacks by suspected herdsmen. At least 16 persons were confirmed dead following separate attacks

item.title: INEC deadline: Parties report progress, raise concerns over candidates’ upload
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Henry Umoru, Abuja Political parties have given updates on the upload of their presidential and National Assembly candidates to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal following the expiration of the July 11, 2026 deadline for submission. While the National Democratic Congress

item.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

item.title: Official: Chippa United announce Stanley Nwabali’s return 5 months after surprise exit
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: South African Premier Soccer League club, Chippa United, has announced the signing of Super Eagles goalkeeper Stanley Nwabali, Soccernet.ng reports. Recall that Nwabali left the Chilli Boys after the end of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations in February this year. Following that move, he was linked with

item.title: Oyedepo Urges Christians To Get PVCs, Determine Who Governs Them
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The founder of Living Faith Church Worldwide, David Oyedepo, has urged Nigerians, particularly Christians, to obtain their Permanent Voter Cards (PVCs) ahead of the 2027 general elections, stressing that voting is a civic responsibility and a constitutional right. Speaking during a Sunday service at

item.title: Volker Sees NATO Summit Strengthening Alliance
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Former US ambassador to NATO Kurt Volker says the NATO summit reinforces alliance unity, strengthens support for Ukraine and sends a tougher message to Russian President Vladimir Putin, while praising President Donald Trump's more constructive engagement with President Volodymyr Zelensky. Volker say

item.title: Iran war: US strategic success or diplomatic failure?

item.description: Marc Lamont Hill challenges former United States diplomat Heino Klinck on the US-Israeli war in Iran.

item.title: Lindsey Graham: 'He was the one who justified Israel's actions'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Graham often advised Trump on foreign affairs, particularly on matters pertaining to Israel, Ukraine and Iran. He was a frequent visitor at the White House. Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

item.title: Gov Lawal unveils $200m Zamfara lithium processing plant
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Idris Salisu, Gusau Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State has inaugurated a $200 million lithium mining and processing plant in Boko Village, Zurmi Local Government Area, describing the project as a milestone in the state’s drive for industrialisation and economic development. The Zamfara L

item.title: Joke Silva Celebrates Olu Jacobs As Veteran Actor Turns 84
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Joke Silva is celebrating her husband, Olu Jacobs, as the legendary actor marks his 84th birthday today, July 12. Taking to Instagram, Silva shared an AI-generated video in honour of her husband and expressed heartfelt gratitude for the milestone. Referring to him as the “L

item.title: SON Opens New Frontier For Nigeria’s Food Export Drive
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigeria has strengthened its position in global food standards development after securing international approval for a new standard on root and tuber flours, a breakthrough expected to enhance food safety, boost agricultural exports and unlock new opportunities in the global gluten-free food market.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Day 31
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Basel Ghazoghli has your #FIFAworldcup26 day 31 recap, as England and Argentina reach the semi-finals.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Alfie Haaland, the father of Erling Haaland, has said that England’s 2-1 victory over Norway in the quarterfinals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup was a robbery. He said that the Norwegian team was subjected to numerous unfair refereeing decisions throughout the game. The 53-year-old continued by st

item.title: United States at 250: Seven tests of the American experiment
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Seven stories test America’s promises of freedom, opportunity and power as the United States marks its 250th year.

item.title: Operation Kosaye: Police arrest 43 suspects, recover hard drugs in Ogun
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Operatives of the Special Weapons and Tactics, SWAT, Unit of the Ogun State Police Command have arrested 43 more suspects as part of the ongoing Operation Kosaye (no chance). The suspects were arrested during a coordinated raid on a notorious criminal hideout, leading to the recovery of hard drugs,1

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Lagos State Police Command has appealed to members of the public for assistance in locating the family of an 83-year-old man, Mr Oriowo Gabriel, who is currently in police protective custody. In a statement issued on Sunday, the command said the elderly man was found by a compassionate member of

item.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor

item.title: Super Eagles Goalkeeper Nwabali Returns To Chippa United
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Super Eagles goalkeeper Stanley Nwabali has returned to South African club Chippa United, five months after leaving the club by mutual consent. Chippa United confirmed the shot-stopper’s return in a post on its official X account on Sunday, welcoming him back to the Eastern Cape outfit. &#8220

item.title: 2027: Delta NDC guber candidate accuses APC of thuggery
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governorship candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, in Delta State, Deacon Chris Iyovwaye, has accused the Delta State chapter of the All Progressives Congress, APC, of sponsoring thugs who attempted to prevent him and his supporters from accessing a community town hall. The NDC candidat

item.title: Atiku Knocks WAEC, NECO Fee Increment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The flag bearer of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in the 2027 presidential election, Atiku Abubakar, has condemned the recent approval of a uniform N50,000 examination fee for candidates sitting the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) and National Examinations Council (NECO) examination

item.title: Cubana Chief Priest Compares Marriage To Jay-Z And Beyoncé’s Romance
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian socialite Cubana Chief Priest has compared himself and his wife, Angelica Okechukwu, to global music power couple Jay-Z and Beyoncé. Sharing loved-up photos of the pair on Instagram, the celebrity barman proudly described them as the “Jay Z and Beyoncé Ndi Owerri,” celebrating their relatio

item.title: Kano man arrested for allegedly robbing tricycle rider
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kano State Anti-Phone Snatching Force has arrested a young man, identified as Usman Sulaiman of Dorayi Quarters, over the alleged robbery of a commercial tricycle rider in the Panshekara area of Kano. The spokesperson for the task force, Ibrahim Garba Ahmad, disclosed the arrest during an interv

item.title: Spain ex-PM draws criticism with 'xenophobic' remark on French team
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain's conservative ex-prime minister Mariano Rajoy faced criticism at home and in France Sunday after saying the neighbouring country's national football team had "no French players". Our correspondent Sarah Morris tells us more.

item.description: At least eighty-six Nigerians have benefited from a scholarship programme initiated through a strategic partnership between the The post 86 Nigerians benefit from UNESCO REF, UNICAF scholarship awards appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Sanchez slams 'xenophobic' remarks by ex-PM about French football team
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Sunday condemned a remark made by conservative ex-prime minister Mariano Rajoy in which he said that France's national football team had "no French players". Sanchez called the comment 'xenophobic' and several French politicians have also slammed Rajoy.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Popular Nigerian rapper Tochukwu Ojogwu, known professionally as Odumodublvck, has shared the only regret he has regarding his successful music career.   The rapper, who has previously stated that he turned to music after his initial dream of becoming a professional footballer failed, explained

item.title: U-17 WWC: Flamingos To Face Poland, Puerto Rico, Korea In Group B
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Flamingos will face Poland, Puerto Rico and Korea DPR in Group B at the 2026 FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup, Completesports.com reports. Akeem Busari’s side qualified for the competition following a 8-5 on aggregate victory over the Young Amazons of Benin. The Flamingos clai

item.title: Oil Market Prices In Hormuz Resilience
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg Intelligence senior commodities strategist Mike McGlone says oil markets are largely accepting US assurances that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, with expanding Western Hemisphere supply and alternative export routes limiting the long-term impact of Gulf disruptions on crude prices. He

item.title: Tank farm operators, unions responsible for Mile 2–Apapa traffic gridlock — Police

item.description: By Efe Onodjae The Ports Authority Police (Western Command) has distanced itself from the persistent gridlock caused by petroleum tankers along the Mile 2–Apapa–Kirikiri corridor, saying the root causes of the traffic congestion lie beyond the powers of the Police. item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigeria’s healthcare sector received a major boost on Friday as Dangote Cement Plc commissioned a multimillion naira hospital in Obajana, Kogi State. item.title: NBA confirms 82,213 eligible voters ahead of July 18 Poll
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Voting will begin at 12:00 a.m. on July 18 and end at 11:59 p.m. the same day. The results are expected to be announced on July 19.

item.title: Deputy Gov Lauds Imo SACA For HIV Advocacy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Imo State deputy governor, Lady Chinyere Ihuoma Ekomaru, has commended the Imo State Agency for the Control of AIDS (IMSACA), for its sustained efforts in HIV/AIDS awareness campaigns and support for persons living with the disease. Lady Ekomaru gave the commendation at Government House, Owerri,

item.title: Gunmen Kill 9 Family Members, 2-Month-Old Baby In Plateau Communities
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Gunmen killed nine members of one family, including a two-month-old baby in a fresh attack on Kum and Wereng-Camp communities in Riyom Local Government Area of Plateau State at the weekend. The attack, according to residents occured at about 11:30 p.m. on Saturday and lasted for more than one hour,

item.title: Mathieu Van der Poel overcomes heat to win Tour de France stage nine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: 31-year-old Dutchman and cobbled classics specialist Mathieu van der Poel claimed his third Tour de France win on Sunday with victory in the ninth stage, which was shortened due to intense heat. The former world champion, won a sprint amongst his three breakaway companions with Tobias Johannessen ta

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

item.description: Brendon McCullum's departure from the role of England Test head coach leaves the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) with a huge gap to fill ahead of a crucial home Ashes series next summer.

item.title: Navy Chief Backs Bigger NATO Role
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle says NATO's naval alliances remain strong as the US shifts more responsibility to allies while focusing on capabilities only the US Navy can provide. He says the Navy is accelerating autonomous systems, preparing for growing competition in the Arctic and p

item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.description: The faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) loyal to the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike has uploaded its candidates for the presidential and National Assembly elections to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal. item.title: World Cup 2026: Erling Haaland makes promise after England eliminate Norway
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Norway striker, Erling Haaland, has vowed to keep working hard, improve himself and do everything he can to help his team achieve better results going forward. Haaland spoke at his post-match interview after England eliminated Norway from the World Cup in a quarter-final encounter on Saturday. Recal

item.title: Troops Intercept Truck Loaded With Illicit Drugs On Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army‘s 65 Battalion have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of illicit drugs along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway following actionable intelligence from a credible source. According to a statement by the Acting Deputy Director, Army Public Relations, 81

item.title: Atiku criticises proposed ₦50,000 WAEC, NECO examination fees
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Former Vice President and African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has criticised the Federal Government over the proposed ₦50,000 examination fee for candidates sitting WAEC and NECO examinations from 2027.

item.title: 2027: INEC portal becomes first test of parties’ readiness
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …PDP confirms upload of presidential, NASS candidates as INEC extends nomination deadline to July 14 …ADC, PRP report smooth exercise …Other parties battle procedural hurdles By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) says it has successfully uploaded the names of its president

item.title: Conference 57 CPS Honours Akosile For Championing Strategic Communication
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Special Adviser on Media and Publicity to the Lagos State Governor, Mr. Gboyega Akosile, has been honoured with an Award of Excellence by the Conference 57 Chief Press Secretaries (CPS) for his contributions to strategic communication and public information management. Conference 57 CPS is the umbre

item.title: Peller, Jarvis announce wedding date
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Habeeb Hamzat, popularly known as Peller and his partner, Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, known as Jarvis, have announced readiness to get married... The post Peller, Jarvis announce wedding date appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: How to Read More Books

item.title: US senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies aged 71
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham, an influential ally of President Donald Trump and advocate of hawkish US foreign policy, has died unexpectedly aged 71, his office said on Sunday. Graham was a staunch supporter of the Iran war, and in recent years urged both Trump and the Biden administrations to back K

item.title: Oriire abduction: Rescued principal’s husband hails Tinubu, Makinde after wife’s freedom
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The family of Professor Wole Alamu has expressed deep appreciation to the Almighty God and to all Nigerians (home and abroad) for The post Oriire abduction: Rescued principal’s husband hails Tinubu, Makinde after wife’s freedom appeared first on Tribune Online.
